description: abc-nodes cluster monitoring: Nomad jobs, node resources, alloc metrics, group/user fairness, per-research-user views (job names: script-job-<institute>-<department>-<group>_<user>--…), and logs

panels.description: Active allocations per namespace (from Nomad client metrics — correlates with workload presence).
panels.title: Active allocations by namespace

panels.description: Failed job summary gauge by namespace (Prometheus — complements Loki stderr panels below).
panels.title: Failed jobs by namespace (summary)
Group Fairness — Resource Share by Namespace

panels.description: CPU MHz currently allocated per research group namespace. A dominant slice means that group is monopolising the cluster.
panels.title: CPU Share by Group (now)

panels.description: Memory (MB) currently allocated per research group namespace.
panels.title: Memory Share by Group (now)

panels.description: Number of running task group allocations per namespace. Imbalance signals group monopolisation.
panels.title: Running Allocations by Group (now)

panels.description: CPU usage trend per namespace over time — reveals if one group is consistently crowding others.
panels.title: CPU Allocation Trend by Group

panels.description: Memory allocation trend per namespace over time.
panels.title: Memory Allocation Trend by Group

panels.description: Queued allocations per namespace — a persistent queue means that group cannot schedule and may be starving the others.
panels.title: Queued Allocations by Group (scheduler pressure)

panels.description: Preempted allocations — Nomad has evicted lower-priority jobs to make room. Non-zero means fairness enforcement is working.
panels.title: Preempted Allocations (fairness enforcement)

panels.description: Filtered by the Research user variable. Legend = institute-dept-group_user parsed from job name.
panels.title: Running / complete / failed jobs (filtered by research user)
Alloc memory RSS (filtered by research user)

panels.description: Matches log lines emitted by abc job run workloads: research_user=<slug> …
panels.title: Workload logs (research_user tag in stdout)

panels.description: Live log stream from all allocations across the cluster. Use alloc_id and task filters above to narrow scope.
panels.title: All Cluster Logs (alloc=$alloc_id  task=$task)

title: abc-nodes Cluster Overview
